e1b_threshold.note: E1-b threshold RE-PINNED ON DATA-DRIVEN FRICTION — with the mechanism CORRECTED by measurement: the synthetic well diversity (0.7839) EXCEEDS level-0 (0.7682), so blobbiness-BETWEEN cells was NOT the failure; the failure was INCOHERENCE-WITHIN (P1 held-out halves: own-cell C=0.825 < 1 — batch-authored cells are grab-bags whose halves do not self-compress). The organic accumulation gate is therefore P1-LITE SELF-COHERENCE (held-out C > 1.1) as PRIMARY, diversity-vs-level0 as secondary. Run P1-lite before any full P-run is spent.

intake.note: THE INTAKE RATCHET (2026-07-20). Floors only rise. Measured after the stratified-intake rebuild; the prior narrow intake read 40 commits + ~33 doc files + 40 script comment blocks, front-truncated, identical every tick.
intake.source: scripts/pmu/reef-miner.mjs stratified intake; measured ticks 5-7 → doc 351 · code 1605 · scored 189-240 · novelty_median 0.770-0.784
intake.curation: SIGNAL_DIRS only (docs/architecture|pmu|ops|strategy|incidents + scripts/**); NOISE_RE excludes crm|outreach|comms|leads|contacts|newsletter|book — measured: an uncurated docs/** walk made the novelty ranker surface LinkedIn contact rows
intake.novelty: gzip-NCD min-vs-per-domain-text, used SUBTRACTIVELY (drop most-redundant quartile), never as the ranking key — ranking by novelty maximizes distance from the reef and selects off-topic noise

efficiency.note: THE EFFICIENCY RATCHET (2026-07-20). Placement efficiency = applied / (applied + burned). Floors only rise. The supply-driven miner measured 3.35% (9 applied vs 260 burned at capacity over 43 ticks) — it aimed at full lanes because that is where the repo's text mass sits. reef-demand.mjs inverts this: score lanes by starvation x importance, hunt with the target's own vocabulary, pick by LOWEST gzip-NCD (fit). Every point of this number is reclaimed compute.
efficiency.target: demand-driven (reef-demand.mjs) — the ceiling is 100%: every write fills a verified void

yield.note: THE YIELD RATCHET (2026-07-20). Three numbers, and the hierarchy is the point — each alone is gameable, which is why the PRIMARY is the product.

primary.why_primary: It is the only one that cannot be gamed from either end. Hit rate alone is gamed by writing nothing (no burns). Yield-quality-without-demand is gamed by refilling one easy cell forever. The PRODUCT punishes both: a starved cell with no material scores low (bad fit), and a perfectly-fitting passage for a cell nobody needs scores low (low demand).
primary.blocks_why: nothing gates on it yet — I4 A/B and I5 UI read it. It becomes blocking when the A/B lands, not before: a floor that gates nothing cannot claim to be enforced.
primary.width_why: yield is width-dependent (measured: 0.329@w1 0.398@w3 0.529@w5). A floor is only comparable within its width — the earlier 0.676 vs 0.33 alarm was a w8 floor read against w3 ticks.
primary.source: data/pmu/shootout-ledger.ndjson rows where deflated===true && width===8 (recomputable by anyone)
primary.superseded_why: 0.7725 was pinned on the pre-I2 fixed point: the demand engine returned the identical winner every tick, so 35 readings carried 2 distinct values — effective n=1. Doctrine 5: re-derive on regime change, old floors describe a system that no longer exists. I2 broke the fixed point and the regime changed.
primary.doctrine: pinned at the OBSERVED MINIMUM over the window (never the max — a max-pinned floor refuses the very next cycle), with >=5 DISTINCT values behind it

secondary.why: measures AIMING — it collapses when the loop writes into full cells. This is the 3.54% number the supply-driven regime earned.

diagnostic.why: throughput. Deliberately NOT a floor: a tick that honestly writes nothing (no target under the fit ceiling) is correct behaviour, and flooring throughput would incentivise forced fills — the exact defect being repaired.

width_is_measured_not_guessed.reading: ~350ms per additional target; fit and yield_quality both improve MONOTONICALLY through width 8. Cost is <10% of a 26-75s tick, so width is bounded by demand-drift, never by compute. Width may be ANY integer — 2 and 4 are as valid as 3 and 5. Re-measure as the reef fills: the optimum moves narrower once starved cells finally have supply.
width_is_measured_not_guessed.decision_path_is_on_the_tape: data/pmu/shootout-ledger.ndjson records every contender, its demand score, best fit, and the runners-up' material — so 'why did tick N fill X and not Y' stays answerable, and a later tick can carry over a close second instead of rediscovering it.

PRECISION_DOCTRINE.note: How a floor earns the right to be a floor. Written 2026-07-20 after catching that yield_quality 0.7725 was pinned on the MAX of a DEGENERATE sample (2 distinct values, all from a fixed-point run where the winner never changed — effective n=1). The same session had already established the opposite rule for F1: advance to the observed MIN, never the max.
rules: 1. PIN THE OBSERVED MINIMUM over the window, never the maximum. A max-pinned floor refuses the very next cycle.
2. COUNT DISTINCT VALUES, not samples. 35 identical readings from a fixed point is n=1. A floor needs >=5 DISTINCT observations before it is anything but an estimate.
3. EVERY FLOOR CARRIES ITS PROVENANCE: metric · source artifact (recomputable) · window size · distinct-sample count · comparison operator · what happens on breach (block vs advisory).
4. A FLOOR BELOW ITS DISTINCT-SAMPLE THRESHOLD IS MARKED provisional:true AND MUST NOT BLOCK — it reports as an advisory until it earns its sample.
5. RE-DERIVE, DO NOT INHERIT. When the regime changes (supply-driven -> demand-driven), old floors describe a system that no longer exists. Re-measure or mark stale.
PRECISION_DOCTRINE.applied_now: yield_quality demoted to provisional (2 distinct values from a degenerate run). It reports; it does not block. Re-pin at the observed MIN once I2 breaks the fixed point and >=5 distinct winners have been observed.

cost.metric: neutral_bytes_per_accept = reef byte growth per accepted cycle whose capability delta (dF1 + derived + anchors) is zero
cost.floor_provenance: measured offending rate 10,882 bytes / 12 neutral accepts = 907 bytes per neutral accept; 700 sits below the offending regime and above the healthy regime's noise (an accept that moves capability is exempt — the ceiling prices NEUTRAL mass only)
cost.blocks_why: advisory first, mirroring how yield.primary started — a cost gate that has never been seen to fire correctly should not block on day one; promote to blocking after it has flagged one real accumulation and zero false positives
cost.seated_from: data/pmu/yield-cost-proposal.json (the spec §9 M3 packet, emailed 2026-07-20T17:10Z) — seated under the operator's /goal finish-the-board directive; the floor field carries the ceiling value, direction in the operator
cost.source: tape-health self-audit-usefulness firing 2026-07-20; spec §5 decision 3; spec §9 M3

selectors.metric: count of selectors that rank candidate material without consulting attempt history
selectors.breach_behaviour: BLOCKS — a count above the pin means either a new memoryless selector shipped or a debt tag was deleted without wiring the selector
selectors.undeclared_breach: BLOCKS — an undeclared memoryless selector is an incident, never an advisory
selectors.provenance: the census first run discovered 7 selectors; 2 were wired by I2, 5 carry an at-site // @selector: memoryless-debt tag. NONE of the 5 was on I2 hardcoded list of two — which is the argument for discovery over memory.
selectors.why_not_a_list: the names live as tags in the files themselves (principle: no subsystem keeps an allowlist of its own components). This pin is a COUNT, so it cannot become a roster that quietly grows.
selectors.tape: docs/architecture/self-observation-law-spec.md §7 — the REASONING for each debt entry (what it does, which trap, what breaks if wired early). This pin is the GATE; that section is the TAPE. A gate may not be its own explanation.
